definition: The U.S. scores 2.0-2.5 on a 5-signal fiscal dominance sequence: (i) debt/GDP >100% [YES], (ii) interest expense > defense spending [YES], (iii) implicit Treasury-Fed coordination [DEBATABLE], (iv) regulatory forced buying [NO], (v) inflation de-anchoring [NO]. Japan analogue suggests transition from signal (iii) to (iv) can take 5-15 years for a reserve currency.
evidence: U.S. debt/GDP ~124%, net interest ~$950B vs. defense ~$850B
5Y5Y breakevens ~2.3-2.5% (within range, signal v not triggered)
Japan case: all 5 signals present, gradual over decades
Italy 2011: signals i-iii triggered rapidly, resolved by ECB backstop
UK 2022: attempted to skip from signal i to v, market rejected within days
Fed effectively tightened in 2022-23 despite deficits (challenger_02: partial counter-evidence)
open_questions: Does reserve currency status permanently modify the threshold or merely delay?
Can the Fed ease during fiscal consolidation without amplifying term premium concerns?
How should this be modeled as a continuous probability rather than binary regime?
